[sentiment=POSITIVE | stars=5]
Title: I wouldn’t shop anywhere else for a new TV
- I bought my last TV here 12 years ago and felt like the guys really knew their stuff at the time. So when it broke on Christmas Day Richer Sounds was my immediate thought. With a 6 year guarantee it outshines the likes of John Lewis and the expertise and customer service offered by the staff is exceptional. I was served by the manager and ended up buying a projector instead of a TV. He was absolutely happy to turn the lights off in store and turn up the volume to make sure i was happy before i purchased. If you want to make a good purchase and feel valued while you’re doing it, Richer Sounds should be your next stop

- In a world where customer service seems to be an alien concept to most businesses, Richer Sounds are a beacon! Bought a turntable for my son for Christmas, saved a good amount of money by signing up to the VIP club, and then a small issue was fixed instantly and brilliantly. I actually can’t wait to shop at Richer Sounds again.

[sentiment=NEGATIVE | stars=3]
Title: Poor online experience 
- Like other reviewers, I would agree that the in store experience is very good, however ordering online is a whole different story. I placed a click and collect order for a soundbar a week ago, and the website lead me to believe it would be shipped the next day.After a couple of days I called customer service, to be told that it would be in store on Friday, a week after I placed the order. I appreciate that Christmas fell in between, so I was willing to give them the benefit of the doubt. Needless to say the soundbar didn’t ship, so a week later I called to cancel the order, only to be told there’s a stock issue, and that the cancellation had to be processed by the store, even though the order was placed online. Had the website told me the item was out of stock I wouldn’t have placed the order, but presumably Richer Sounds are only interested in taking your money.Customer service weren’t particularly helpful, so I’m waiting to hear back from the store about the cancellation. Be wary of ordering online from this company. Update - it turns out the item was shipped (despite the website saying it hadn’t) so I was able to pick it up at the store. The store were excellent in sorting everything out so I’ve amended my rating. I still maintain that the online ordering process leaves a lot to be desired.

[sentiment=NEGATIVE | stars=1]
Title: DO NOT USE THIS COMPANY 
- Back and forth with the customer service team to manage a return who are acting like the middle man between the manufacturer and myself, but it isn’t the manufacturer who should be managing my return as I purchased from marks electrical. I still have no clue who I’m supposed to contact regarding my return and collection. On their website, they state small items can be returned in the post at a cost to the consumer, and larger items they will collect at a price of £70+. However, this is in direct violation of the consumer contracts regulation 2013 which states that if an item has been delivered to the consumers home and is too bulky to return via post, the seller must collect it at their own cost. It’s shocking seeing a company of this size using terms and conditions that violate the law. I’m now awaiting a response from the complaints department, but will be raising the matter with the financial ombudsman as they’re in direct violation of consumer rights. Really dissatisfied with the service I’ve been provided and the constant back and forth between myself and the manufacturer who has nothing to do with the return. I won’t be using this supplier again, and hope you don’t too.

Avoid, sells office 365 username and passwords on the cheap rather than license keys, they don't honour warranty, refunds, and sales wording is misleading across the product pages. Support ignored my follow up emails. Chargeback only option.A general point is reselling username and passwords is a very grey area. The details could be hacked or stolen, and the user details you receive could be later banned by Microsoft, which tells you enough about risk with what you're buying. The fact you're told to change the password on receipt is dodgy. The reseller issuing the accounts could also have access to your account. Don't believe the response you'll get on its legitimacy, it's a scam.

- The worst and most dishonest service provider. They claimed among the product features: genuine software, 24/7 support, lifetime warranty, installation files, multi language, no expiration, permanent updates, lifetime account. I still have a screenshot of this product with the guaranteed features from their website. The product only worked for a couple of months, but it's stopped working on a regular basis, and the attached 1 TB OneDrive access was also intermittent. It is failed to synchronize repeatedly, and we frequently lost access to our connected 1TB OneDrive account and all our uploaded contents too. Despite the fact that they provided new sign-in credentials, the problem recurred and persisted for a few months afterward. As it happened again, we were unable to access the purchased product using our most recent sign in credentials, despite their promises of a lifetime account, lifetime warranty, no expiration, and 24/7 support. We were disconnected from our OneDrive account and the Microsoft 365 Home edition sign in option without warning. When we contacted their customer support about this issue on September 17th, 2024, we received no communication or assistance from them. We emailed them again on September 24th, 2024, and requested a refund; they responded to this second email.It appears this company intentionally misleads and scams customers, causing them harm. They never communicated that they purposefully falsely advertised their product on their website in order to mislead customers, and they also failed to mention that the connected 1TB OneDrive account has an expiration date without warning, and the lifetime warranty and 24/7 support are merely a joke. We were unable to retrieve our contents from their cloud services and received no assistance in doing so. It is ridiculous that we paid for their product and did not receive what was promised at the time of the purchase. On top of that, we lost all of our contents. It doesn't sound like an ethical service at all.The only thing we received was a refund at the end. However, we lost nearly all of our contents. So, do yourself a favor and avoid them like the plague!UPDATE: Following the publication of this review, Softwaresupply.net offered us a compensation of 100 Euros for our lost content on the connected inaccessible OneDrive account. Unfortunately, they were unable to assist in retrieving the uploaded and lost data. They also claimed that the above-mentioned problem was caused by one of their suppliers, and Softwaresupply.net states that they stopped cooperating with this supplier.

Hopefully that will get me going with the antenna.EDIT - Based on the response by the team, I assume it's a 'Bot' that populated the response, and nobody actually read it in the team or cares about feed back really 🤔.EDIT 2 - By 'heavy handed' do you mean that I tightened the flimsy clamps too much, or something more cryptic - confused.EDIT 3 - No need to supply alternative clips, the following day from delivery, I purchased various size clips to replace the ones supplied. For reference, the thin stainless ones supplied were open slotted which reduced the strength st the slot walls as there was hooped stress resistance at the slots. I replaced with blind slotted clips which retained the stress capability at the acme thread slots.Therefore now assembled in situ.Perhaps worth mentioning to the supplier to review the clip capability (especially the smaller sizes).Hope that helps.

- Needed to buy a lightweight Vacuum recently having used Dyson and Shark Uprights for many years, I still have my Shark but cannot use it due to the weight of the vacuum at the moment.  I bought the Gtech AirRAM Platinum in September 24 having read reviews saying how good it is! As the machine does not hold much in the canister it does need emptying often and the filter needs cleaning regularly - another exposure to the dust. Some dust and dirt is also left on the machine surface when the canister is initially removed for emptying, as some is left on the machine surface at the base of the machine it could also be getting distributed back into the air as it is vacuuming as its on the outside of the container??I find all of this to be a major fault with this machine and a possible health issue to anyone with allergies as the exposure to the dust and dirt in or surrounding the canister is high!! I certainly expected it to be built to a higher standard than this, for the cost of the product.

- I bought an OLED TV (@£2000) from Richer Sounds with a 6 year guarantee which developed a permanent dark shadow on the screen. The guarantee still had about a year to run, so I checked out the website to learn how to make a claim. You have to contact the manufacturer direct, so I rang the number supplied, but the connection was very bad, so the lady sent an email requesting photos of the fault, details of model ser. no etc. After sending the details she asked for, I received a second email saying she had booked my tv for repair, and an engineer would come round in a couple of days. So the engineer came round, examined the screen and said 'you've got screen burn, they will say that's your own fault, but if you look around the outer edge, there are dead pixels, you will get a new screen for that.' Great I thought, so we removed the tv from the wall, and he took it away. Couple of days later, got a call to say that they could not do a repair as it had screen burn, which is not covered by the guarantee, and would return the tv. So I got a tv, which is about 5 years old, with screen burn, cost about 2 grand, with a worthless 6 year guarantee.

That email was quickly followed by a second email again telling me there was a problem and my offer was now revised to £180!The reasons provided were:- Rear microphone quality issue – report available upon request- Visible scratching around the housing – Images available upon request.Safe to say i immediately rejected their revised offer and requested someone contact me to arrange the return of my phone to me, given i know for a fact the phone was in great condition and i also had zero issues with the microphone.I ended up receiving a call from a manager (the only good part of this experience was that the lady was very friendly and polite).I advised i was disgusted with the emails i had received and their revised offer and that i had had the phone in a solid phone case, with a screen protector, since new and i had also taken photos as proof of it’s condition before sending. The lady quickly went away to review my order. Upon her return, the lady advised me that actually there had been an error and my phone was in “pretty pristine condition” (her words) and that she would make me a revised offer of £270. She maintained that their Apple accredited software had advised there was an issue with the rear microphone so that needed to be replaced and apparently they would have to give the phone a polish to brighten up some dulling. Again i quickly declined the offer and asked for her to arrange the return of my phone, to which she seemed quite shocked that i wanted the phone back! She went away again and then came back with a second revised offer of £310, with the £43 deduction now being for the cost of a replacement microphone only.So in summary, i was originally offered £353, that was knocked down to £180 apparently due to an issue with the microphone (cost if which to replace is £43) and some “scratches”.  All of a sudden after i request my phone is returned and maintain my position, the “scratches” seem to disappear and they can suddenly offer me another £130 on top of their original revised offer.This company is quite clearly trying to rip people off by making up “issues” with devices and then low balling revised offers.When challenged on this, all of a sudden you are then advised that oh actually there was an error and your phone is in “pretty pristine” condition and you are suddenly offered much more than they try to get away with initially.I do not believe for a second that there was an “error” with my order, i simply believe that this company tries to get away with what they can in terms of reducing offers and paying people much less than they believe they will be receiving and then when challenged they fall back on the “error” excuse.I was in a position where i was not by any means desperate to sell the phone and to be quite honest i would have preferred to have given it away than accept an offer i felt was utter robbery by an unscrupulous company but i do fear for those who do need the funds more than i do and who are then getting ripped off by this company!Absolute disgrace and a horrible experience.NEVER AGAIN!!!!!!!!**UPDATE to original review 06/12/24** - Following my earlier experience, i received a further phone call from Nicola the next morning and was advised that she was extremely sorry i had had a bad experience and would like to try to reach a solution that we were both happy with.I was offered compensation which has now put me in a better position than i would have been.Whilst the original experience was not good, the company has since tried to make up for the experience & my dissatisfaction (they reached out to me in order to discuss and try to ensure i was leaving the experience more satisfied and not unhappy).  We have reached a resolution which i am now very happy with & Nicola was very pleasant to deal with throughout.

I was working away from home a lot and thought I had plenty of time to sort it out because it said 10 year warranty on parts. When I finally got in touch I was told nothing could be done unless I was prepared to pay a sum that would have been one third of the original cost because the warranty had run out.Apparently even if I had still been in warranty i would have had to shell out a very large sum to have someone carry out the repair.Then the trivet broke when one of the arms snapped off completely and another one is bent. I can replace that but only if I am prepared to pay around £60. Now both ovens are turning off constantly when I open and close the doors. It beggars belief.My last gas stove was second hand and I had it for 17 years. I estimate its age at betwen 25-30 years. When I needed to replace it, I thought I would go with something bigger and more up to date. I have been saddled with a nightmare.This is made of the shoddiest, cheapest, low quality materials it is possible to sell without contravening safety law. It stinks. It wasn't a cheap product as it cost around £380. I'm sure the shoddiness is a deliberate part of the design as I wouldn't be surprised if Hotpoint make more income from repairing your products than selling them.I will never buy a Hotpoint product again.

Title: Expensive but they can find those old original parts 
- I have a Cannon BHC 120 cooker hood which is probably 20 years old. The lights haven’t worked for maybe ten of those years. I looked at buying a new one and they don’t exist anymore, but the cheapest alternative would be £890 +. So I called in NAC Domestic Appliances who attended on time and diagnosed a faulty transformer and printed circuit board. They tried to find one but unsurprisingly they’re out of production. £96 for the visit. They said if I could find the parts they would come back and fit them for free. The engineer said it was a really high quality hood and you can’t get them anymore. So I searched the internet (and rang my brother - an engineer), who suggested the PCB could be repaired. Back to the internet and I found the transformer available on Ransom Spares (aptly named - they charged £98 but then it is an original). The real find was Electronic Partners. They promised they can repair PCB’s. I don’t know if they’re a clearance house for various electricians but heyho. They sent me a postage label and asked the details. Off it went, then they asked me for the control panel as well (for testing) and then diagnosed the problem and estimated £108. I got it back a week later. They’d replaced the triacs apparently. NAC re attended today and pieced it back together (as they said - for free). It now works as if it were new. Just need to give it a polish now!

Title: Stopped working after 14 months
- Fridge stopped working after 14 months. I contacted them to be told it’s ’out of warranty’. A fridge should last more than 14 months! Lucky we have protection under the Consumer Rights Act so it’s irrelevant that  ‘their’ warranty has expired. The Consumer Rights Act states goods must be of satisfactory quality and last a reasonable time. A fridge only lasting 14 months is neither of satisfactory quality nor lasting a reasonable time.They have now agreed to a repair but I have to return the item in suitable packaging, which I do not have. I would therefore have to purchase packaging when under the Law, it is not supposed to cost me anything.They keep saying it’s a ‘gesture of goodwill’ that they are agreeing to the repair. Let’s be clear, it’s not a gesture of goodwill, it is their legal obligation under the Consumer Rights Act! Make sure you pay on credit card as well so that you have the added protection of being able to issue a section 75 chargeback under the Consumer Credit Act to get your money back if you need to. I will now be issuing the chargeback since I should not be out of pocket to purchase packaging to return the item for repair.

[sentiment=NEGATIVE | stars=2]
Title: 13 Years And I'm Done
- I was with giffgaff for almost 13 years. It was great when it started, affordable rolling plans that didn't lock you in, on what was at the time probably the best network in the UK. But like many companies that grow over time, it has become a victim of its own success and has begun the classic slide into tech company enshitification.First, do not buy a phone from them. Although they brand it as their own service, it is farmed out to a third party over which they have no control. Quality issues seem common, as are logistical ones.Speaking of logistical problems, there was recently an ammonia leak at said third party warehouse which resulted in evacuations and thousands of orders being delayed for weeks. This is obviously something out of their control, but the way they chose to handle it was unacceptable. Whether it was intentionally misleading customers or poor communication with their supplier, they continued taking orders they couldn't meet, did not announce the delays until people started complaining, and then obfuscated the seriousness of them behind vague cut and paste platitudes from customer relations staff who probably weren't told what had happened themselves. I assert that they took a calculated risk that although some people would be annoyed and cancel their orders, they'd still make more money than if they gave people their right to make an informed decision from the start. If it wasn't that, then there was at least a lot of incompetence running around and neither option is a good one. On a more practical day to day level, the quality of the network signal has been steadily declining, possibly as a result of more people joining the network. Obviously this is different from region to region and you should always check which of the 4 networks is best for your area before choosing, but for me it is no longer this one.

[sentiment=NEGATIVE | stars=2]
Title: LTSC is not what I purchased
- I bought a license for Microsoft Office Professional Plus 2024 and selected the said product from the website downloads list mentioned in the instructions. When it downloaded and activated it, It was Microsoft Office LTSC Professional Plus 2024. There are differences. Features: Both versions include a subset of the features in Microsoft 365 Apps, such as improved accessibility, new capabilities, and performance improvements. However, Office LTSC Professional Plus 2024 doesn't include new features and functionality that are available in Microsoft 365 Apps. Licensing: Office Professional Plus is only available through volume licensing channels. Office LTSC Professional Plus 2024 is a perpetually licensed version of Microsoft Office. Updates: Office LTSC Professional Plus 2024 doesn't receive new feature updates throughout its lifecycle. Microsoft supports each LTSC release for five years. Recommended use: Office LTSC Professional Plus 2024 is recommended for computers that are shared by multiple users. Individual users should install the Microsoft Office 365 applications. Included applications: Office LTSC Professional Plus 2024 includes Outlook, Word, Excel, and PowerPoint. However, the Microsoft Publisher app is not included in Office LTSC 2024 because it has been retired. The Teams application is also not installed, but can be downloaded and installed separately.

Title: Zeiss Secacam 7

Set up was very simple straight out of the box and being being able to have images sent to my phone is a lot of fun as it lets you see what the camera is picking up in real time.

Title: Mathematical textbooks can have serious legibility issues
- Somewhat disappointed by the textbooks in my area of interest (mathematics and statistics). Some take a long time to load and search, and algebra is presented in tiny, virtually unreadable jpgs. UPDATE:  Perlego are taking this issue seriously, and are trying to get publishers to give them PDFs as well as ePUB versions, the former displaying mathematical content perfectly well. Ironically, the older textbooks are less likely to cause problems, as they're more often in PDF format. As Hayley at Perlego helpfully pointed out to me, equations in ePUBeditions can be made a bit more legible simply by zooming in (for example by using "ctrl" and "+")I've amended my rating accordingly.

[sentiment=NEGATIVE | stars=2]
Title: Fantastic concept, poorly executed
- Fantastic concept, poorly executed. Perlego offers access to a sizeable range of academic books for a fair annual price. I started using the service readily, excited by the prospect of reading some previously unaccessible titles due to their cost. Sadly, the user experience on safari browser on my MacBook, safari browser on my iPad Pro and the iPad Pro app have been really very poor. Compared to similar ebook either online browsing or apps, Perlego is simply shocking and miles behind an acceptable standard. Across all three viewing platforms mentioned above, it is incredibly difficult to navigate around an ebook. Books are also slow to load. On a Mac using the online ereader, if you adjust the window size even slightly, the whole thing including text size dramatically changes and you lose your place instantly. There is this weird progress X of X at the bottom left which doesn't make any sense compared to the book length. You can't look up (define) words by clicking on it as you would browsing online or on other ereader platforms - rather it simply highlights the text (difficult if the book isn't in your first language) (on the iPad safari browser it doesn't even highlight properly). Really disappointing for what should be a great concept, poorly let down by the user experience. Come on developers, not good enough!EDIT: reply to Perlego's reply. But the experience is poor even on a Macbook, as stated above, it isn't much better than a tablet. When I mean lookup words I mean get a dictionary definition for.

Title: Displays text and graphics, but the toolset lacks modern functionality, process flow and stability.
- Hi, I am a university lecturer and I'm required to use an eBook for one of my courses. The faculty online resource library uses Perlego as the platform of choice for this textbook. First problem: the Perlego app does not "yet" offer an iPad version of the textbook (and I got a prompt apologetic email from customer support, thank you - glad to know you don't avoid hearing criticism), so I am limited to accessing it on my laptop - which I primarily use to run powerpoint and media files while lecturing. OK, not a disaster, but seriously inconvenient. Next problem: I like to highlight text, colour coded, and add note boxes to help me navigate between the text and the presentation slides and to add marginal note boxes and comments for myself and the students. This is possible with Perlego, but the highlight and de-highlight functions are cumbersome, sporadically capturing huge blocks of text which I have to then de-highlight if I release my mouse in the wrong millisecond time box.  To de-highlight, I must open the Chapter pulldown menu on the left margin of the screen, click "Annotations" if not already open, scroll down to a short text string that is a snippet of my target text, and click an X to the right of the string. And if I want to de-hi multiple text blocks I need to know which snippet belongs to which text block. Finally I click back into the body of the text and resume my reading and markups. Most e-readers I've used make this process much simpler (iBooks, Bookshelf, Kindle, PDF...). For me this has a significant impact on my flow, i.e. efficiency. Too much clicking and no keystroke functionality.  But my added text boxes are stable. What also disappoints is the lack of an underline function (which other readers have, too). Third problem: seems to me that if I dwell on a page too long, the infinite scroll scrolls but the text does not load. The workaround here is to go back to my library (click the big P) and re-open the book ("continue reading").  Happily it opens on the last page I used, and my markups are uncorrupted. Fourth Problem: this has been mentioned by other reviewers... not being able to search within the book is absolutely unconscionable, as it is one of the most important and most used functions of any modern e-reader. so... go to the Chapter pulldown, select Index, scroll through the index for the right keywords or topic... one redeeming quality here is that I can click on the page number and go right to the page containing the indexed topic. Very often it's convenient to be able to find all instances of a text string, like "value chain mapping." And of course - I know I'm demanding a lot - after doing the in-book search string it's nice to have a one-click or key combo to go back to where I left off reading before the search.I know that a lot of resources and know-how go into making a user friendly e-reader, so I really hope Perlingo can prioritise and develop new releases to improve functionality with frequent releases. The way it performs for me, as it is now, tells me any other e-reader I know of would provide a better experience.Now, I've only made as far as page 16 in the textbook, so far... but I hope I've uncovered my main issues and my efficiency might pick up a bit.OVERALL: a poor competitor to other e-readers, too much reliance on mouse clicks, no discernible keystroke functions, jittery highlighting navigation, NO SEARCH. Focus on process optimisation!! Friendly customer service.

The only reason it went past the 14 day window is because we were trying to get it working, obviously we should have just sent it straight back for a refund (minus their 20% restocking fee) as soon as we got it and it didn't seem to work.I am sure that company is ok when everything goes well and the product is working, but when something is not working they refuse to take any responsibility and just quote their policy, even when it's clear something isn't working. They refused to be reasonable at any point. Their idea of reasonable is offering to sell us a more expensive headset, only then would they allow the none-working one to be returned.If you are confident that the product you are buying will work (which you cant be when buying from a distance), and you are happy with a 20% restocking fee within 14 days if you are not happy with the item, and you are happy that if it breaks then they will refuse to refund you, then use the company.If you don't want that risk, go somewhere where they have a decent returns policy.Reading the reviews, most of the good reviews seem to be from customers where the order has gone well.A lot of the negative ones, customers are complaining about their service. If you notice the companies replies they are all very sharp and defensive, unapologetic, blame the customer and just state that their customer service is #1. It isn't. Read their T&C online and you will get an idea that they really don't want to deal with returns and make it as hard work as possible. Buy elsewhere.

Title: Two thumbs down for these lot
